page: Microsoft Azure B4s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bsv2 server family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading (2 threads per core). The processor includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates that the B4s_v2 delivers moderate single-core performance but exhibits limitations under multi-threaded workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 1894.89 and Geekbench single-core score of 1674.0 both fall within the average range. However, multi-core metrics are weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3869.77 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3558.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only small block transfer rate of 208.14 GB/sec and a large block rate of 29.32 GB/sec, though the write large block bandwidth is average at 25.64 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform reasonably well, as evidenced by an average Passmark CPU encryption score of 3658.04 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 28.71 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor at 19.7 GB/sec.

As a burstable instance, the B4s_v2 offers a cost-effective profile for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the burstable CPU allocation model, which is suitable for handling occasional performance spikes but is not designed for sustained high-compute tasks, as reflected in its lower multi-core and database benchmark scores. This instance is best suited for low-traffic web servers, development and testing environments, light background tasks, and small-scale applications that benefit from a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure B4s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine in the Bsv2 family, configured with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 48 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1674.0, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The burstable CPU allocation makes this instance a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, light web serving, and small-scale background processes that do not require sustained high-compute performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B4s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, designed for cost-effective general-purpose and development workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B4s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, ideal for light general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A burstable, cost-efficient virtual machine with balanced memory and Intel Xeon processing for variable, low-to-moderate computing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Burstable CPU allocation for cost-efficient handling of variable workloads
Average single-core performance suitable for light computing tasks
No local storage or integrated GPU resources included
Best suited for development environments, testing, and low-traffic web serving
